title: Goran Beviin
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Goran}} {{Image}} {{Character |type=Mandalorian |image= |name=Goran Beviin |homeworld=[[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]'' |birth=Between [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]-[[16 BBY/Legends|16 BBY]]''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'', which is set in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], states that Beviin is in his "late fifties". |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'' |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Gray]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=[[Clan Beviin]] |parents= |partners=[[Medrit Vasur]]{{CSWECite|I|72|Beviin, Goran}}{{CSWECite|III|297|Vasur, Medrit}} |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] *[[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]]''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I exercise… prudence.|Goran Beviin|}} '''Goran Beviin''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] commando, a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], and a [[Mercenary/Legends|contract]] [[Assassin/Legends|killer]] active under the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and into the early years of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]]. In [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]], Beviin was on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] when he met a man named [[Nom Anor|Udelen]], who turned out to be a [[Praetorite Vong|vanguard]] for an invading extragalactic [[Species/Legends|species]], the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. The Mandalorians made a deal with the Yuuzhan Vong, becoming a hired hand for the Yuuzhan Vong's ensuing attack on [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. However, after Beviin and the Mandalorians disobeyed an order to capture a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] named [[Kubariet]] on [[New Holgha]], the Mandalorians began working against the Yuuzhan Vong. Beviin fought in the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] alongside Kubariet, and afterward, settled down on his [[Farm/Legends|farm]] on the planet [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] with his [[Marriage/Legends|husband]], [[Medrit Vasur]]. He was one of [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett's]] most trusted [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenants]], and was once called upon to be interim Mandalore while Fett was away. In [[41 ABY]], Beviin helped Fett in the training of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] to defeat her [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] brother, [[Darth Caedus]]. ==Biography== ===Early life=== [[File:JaingHead.svg|thumb|right|200px|A [[Mythosaur/Legends|mythosaur]] symbol, representing Goran Beviin's Mandalorian heritage]] Goran Beviin was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] between [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] and [[16 BBY/Legends|16 BBY]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] as a member of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] culture. Beviin was alive during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the Mandalorian [[Fenn Shysa]], and remembered how Shysa pulled together the Mandalorians after the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] presence on Mandalore. At some point, he met blacksmith [[Medrit Vasur]] and the two men [[Marriage/Legends|married]], living on the [[Beviin-Vasur farm]] near the Mandalorian [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]]. ===Working for the Yuuzhan Vong=== {{Quote|I want to hunt down every last crab–boy in the galaxy. Whether you have a deal with them or not, ''Mand{{'}}alor''.|Goran Beviin|}} Beviin was in the [[Bar Jaraniz]] on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] when he was approached by a man named [[Nom Anor|Udelen]]. Udelen sought to [[Death/Legends|kill]] an opposition leader on [[Ter Abbes]] named [[Tholote B'Leph]]. According to Udelen, B'Leph was a bribe-taker who had an unnatural generosity in awarding government contracts when in power, and Udelen wanted him removed before the next month's elections. Udelen promised Beviin a hundred thousand [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Uneasy about assassinating an opposition politico and the unrest it would cause on Ter Abbes, Beviin left the bar for his [[Gladiator-class assault fighter|Gladiator assault fighter]], ''[[Beroya]]'', and contacted [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]. Fett told Beviin to take the contract, knowing Beviin was facing financial difficulties at the time. Beviin returned to the bar and accepted the deal. [[File:Nom_anor1.jpg|thumb|left|240px|Nom Anor, a vanguard of the Yuuzhan Vong, concealed his true identity to hire Goran Beviin.]] On Nar Shaddaa, Beviin met two Mandalorian females, a widow named [[Briika Jeban]], and her daughter, [[Dinua Jeban|Dinua]], and Beviin decided to take care of the two. Briika and Dinua accompanied Beviin to a rendezvous with Udelen's forces. While Beviin and a group of other Mandalorians, including Boba Fett, waited for Udelen, they were ambushed by a type of fleet that had not been seen before. The strange [[Starship/Legends|ships]] were under the command of Udelen, who revealed himself as Nom Anor, a [[Praetorite Vong|vanguard]] of the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], an extragalactic [[Species/Legends|species]] that intended to take control of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The Yuuzhan Vong allowed Beviin and Fett to board the fleet's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] for negotiations. Fett told Beviin to stay aware on the Yuuzhan Vong's organic ship, and take small pieces of the organic technology found there. While on board, Anor told Beviin and Fett that the Mandalorians had little choice but to assist the Yuuzhan Vong with their conquest of the galaxy. Anor promised good pay for the Mandalorians' help, and Fett accepted, with the condition that the Yuuzhan Vong would leave the [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalore sector]] alone in the ensuing [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]]. Anor then tasked the Mandalorians with securing a landing point for the Yuuzhan Vong on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world of [[Birgis]]. One week after the [[Battle of Helska IV|invasion]] of the planet''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' [[Helska IV]] by the Yuuzhan Vong, Beviin and the Mandalorians attacked Birgis' [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]]. As soon as the commandos landed, Fett made a large water tower near the spaceport explode, distracting the guards and personnel. From there, Beviin and the Mandalorians rushed into the main building of the spaceport, securing it for the Yuuzhan Vong [[Battle of Birgis|ground invasion]]. After the [[Battle of New Holgha]], Yuuzhan Vong [[subaltern]] [[Bur'lorr]] ordered Beviin and the Mandalorians to find a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] named [[Kubariet]], who was still down on the planet [[New Holgha]], and take him alive. The Mandalorians found him without much trouble, beaten and battered on a rooftop. However, they disobeyed their orders, and, realizing that Mandalore had no future with the Yuuzhan Vong, allowed the Jedi to escape. A nearby Yuuzhan Vong warrior attacked Beviin after the Mandalorians disobeyed their orders; however, the Mandalorians attacked and killed the warrior, although Briika Jeban was killed in the battle, which officially ended their short-lived affiliation with the Yuuzhan Vong. Beviin adopted Dinua after her mother's death. Beviin fought in the Yuuzhan Vong War with Boba Fett, the Mandalorians, and Kubariet and was involved in the [[Battle of Mandalore (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Mandalore]] where the Yuuzhan Vong were defeated, despite heavy casualties for the [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]]. ===Settled down=== {{Quote|No heir, no clan, no sense of duty. You're not Mandalorian. You just wear the armor.|Goran Beviin, to Boba Fett|}} After the Yuuzhan Vong War, Beviin settled down on Mandalore with his husband, Medrit Vasur, and their daughter, Dinua. Eventually, he had become one of Boba Fett's most trusted lieutenants. In 40 ABY,''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'' to [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]]. Beviin was approached by [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] politician [[Thrackan Sal-Solo]], who offered him a contract to kill the whole [[Solo family/Legends|Solo family]]. Beviin refused, stating that he did not kill families, just single targets. He relayed this to Boba Fett on the planet [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], along with news about Fett's family; Fett's daughter, [[Ailyn Vel]], was alive, working under the name Ailyn Habuur. Fett was not previously sure of his daughter's fate; she had hunted him for some time, angry about him leaving her and her mother, [[Sintas Vel]], but had not encountered Fett for years. [[File:BobaFettEAtlas.jpg|thumb|right|280px|Boba Fett regarded Goran Beviin as his most trusted lieutenant.]] Later, Beviin was contacted by Boba Fett, who informed Beviin that Sal-Solo wanted the Mandalorians to help defend [[Centerpoint Station]]. Beviin was ordered to assemble as many commandos as he could and meet Fett in two days on [[Drall (planet)|Drall]]. After those two days passed, Beviin had been able to assemble half a dozen Mandalorians. During the Drall meeting, Beviin told Fett that he was a poor ''Mand'alor'', that after the devastation of the Yuuzhan Vong War, the Mandalorians needed more than just a figurehead. He told Fett of a man named [[Venku Skirata|Kad'ika]], who Beviin believed to be a suitable ''Mand'alor''. After the assassination of Thrackan Sal-Solo, Beviin informed Fett of the death of Ailyn Vel. Later that year, Boba Fett left Mandalore for a few days, and appointed Beviin, his most trusted lieutenant, as acting ''Mand'alor'' in his stead. During his short time in charge, Beviin mostly stayed home, shoveling dung and running his [[Farm/Legends|farm]], which had not been doing well as of late. When Fett returned, he told Beviin one of his biggest secrets, revealing that he was terminally ill, and was not going to live much longer. At the same time, Beviin revealed that drillers had found a large load of [[Beskar/Legends|''beskar'']], Mandalorian iron, in the ground, and the newfound ''beskar'' could bring about a time of great economic growth for Mandalore.''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' The ill Fett stayed at Beviin's farm for a while after this revelation, and, due to a DNA sample from [[Jaing Skirata]], was a transfusion away from being healed. Beviin, along with Fett's granddaughter [[Mirta Gev]] and her fiancée [[Ghes Orade]], visited Fett when he had his life-saving transfusion, and once Fett's surgery was done, Beviin showed him the ''[[Bes'uliik]]'', a new [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] made from the load of Mandalorian iron that had been recently found. ===Training Jaina Solo=== {{Quote|My enemy's enemy is my friend, right?''"
"''My enemy's always my enemy but we can both get smart and put that aside while we deal with a common threat.|Jaina Solo and Goran Beviin|}} [[File:JainaSolo-NEGTC.png|thumb|left|280px|Goran Beviin was one of many Mandalorians who trained Jedi Knight Jaina Solo.]] In 40 ABY,''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' to [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]]. the still-living body of Boba Fett's long-missing ex-wife Sintas Vel was found encased in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]. Beviin was present when [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Beluine]] released her from her carbonite [[Prison/Legends|prison]] and welcomed Vel as a guest at his farm, giving her a temporary home as she recovered from the amnesia, blindness, and other side effects of her years trapped in carbonite. When [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] came to Mandalore seeking Fett's training to kill her [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] brother, [[Darth Caedus]], Beviin greeted her at the [[Oyu'baat]] [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcafe]] in the city of Keldabe. Beviin went on to become one of many Mandalorians to train Solo, also allowing the Jedi to stay at his farm while she was in training. Some time after Solo's arrival, Beviin took her to former Mandalore [[Fenn Shysa's memorial]], and taught her some Mandalorian history. He then had a training duel with the Jedi, where Beviin flattened Solo. They fought several more times, with Beviin overwhelming her, forcing her to resort to using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Beviin told Solo after these training bouts to be totally driven on finishing her opponent off, to be a different Jaina. Solo disagreed, believing that it was hate that Beviin was teaching her, but Beviin told her that it was not hate; it was total war. Even after Beviin reassured her of this, she still believed that his fighting style and lessons were of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. Around this time Beviin also attended the wedding party of Mirta Gev and Ghes Orade. During the [[Second Battle of Roche]], a group of Mandalorians were sent into the battle to kill a group of Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]]. Beviin was ordered to stay behind on Mandalore, and remained there until the end of the [[Second Galactic Civil War]].''[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible]]'' ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Nothing I like better than killing crab-boys.|Goran Beviin|}} Beviin was a male Human who had [[Color/Legends|gray]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. He lived with his husband, Medrit Vasur and the couple later adopted a daughter, Dinua Jeban. Beviin was a family man and was welcoming of guests on his farm. Boba Fett thought of Beviin as all heart and courage, and not afraid of the pain of love. He was the closest the anti-social Fett had to a friend. Fett thought that Beviin would make a good ''Mand'alor'', but Beviin did not want to lead and would rather stay on his farm. He held a deep hatred for the Yuuzhan Vong and advocated killing every one. He also held a dislike for the Jedi and described Kubariet as "the only Jedi I've ever trusted." ==Equipment== [[File:Beskad-BHC.png|thumb|right|200px|An example of a ''beskad'', a type of weapon that Goran Beviin used]] Beviin was known as something of a traditionalist and used old-fashioned equipment, including ''beskar'' armor that was colored blue and a Mandalorian [[Sword/Legends|saber]] called a [[Beskad/Legends|''beskad'']]. He also used highly illegal [[Crushgaunt/Legends|crushgaunts]]. ==Behind the scenes== The character of Goran Beviin was created by author [[Karen Traviss]] for her e-book, ''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]'', in [[August]] [[2006]]. His name derives from the [[Mando'a/Legends|Mando'a]] words ''"goran"'' for "smith" and ''"beviin"'' for "lance." Although Beviin's husband, Medrit Vasur, was first mentioned in ''Boba Fett: A Practical Man'', he was not specified as male until his first appearance in ''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'', by Karen Traviss. Vasur was described as Beviin's partner in Traviss' [[2008]] novel ''Legacy of the Force: Revelations'', and by [[September]] of that year, she stated her intention that Beviin and Vasur were married, making them one of the first openly gay couples in ''[[Star Wars]]'' literature.{{WebCite|url=https://www.karentraviss.com/page22/files/Is_it_true_that_some_of_the_Man.html|text=Karen Traviss – frequently asked questions|author=Traviss, Karen|work=KarenTraviss.com|archivedate=20201202070451}} In [[December]] 2008, ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' confirmed their [[in-universe]] marriage. ==Appearances== *''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'' *''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' *''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'' *''[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible]]'' ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{WebCite|url=https://www.karentraviss.com/page22/files/Is_it_true_that_some_of_the_Man.html|text=Karen Traviss – frequently asked questions|work=[https://www.karentraviss.com KarenTraviss.com]|archivedate=20201202070451}} {{Interlang |de=Goran Beviin |pl=Goran Beviin }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Beviin, Goran}} [[Category:Bounty hunters]] [[Category:Clan Beviin]] [[Category:Criminals]] [[Category:Farmers]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:LGBTQIA+ individuals]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Mandalorian Protectors]]
